title: Positive, negative and screenshot proof for Compliance Review, Block and Release

source_definition.task: Validate process behavior, no-overclaim rules, role/payload boundaries and visual long-screen improvements for Compliance Review, Block and Release.
source_definition.goal: Prove this domain can be accepted as process-first implementation slice.
definition_of_done: Screenshots show improved structure or approved exception.

evidence: tests/recommendation-review-workflow-api.spec.ts positive compliance release path persists after gates pass.
tests/compliance-review-release-contract.spec.ts verifies S038-S042 ownership and DOMAIN-G process coverage.
tests/compliance-review-release-ui.spec.ts verifies route-level S038-S042 contract anchors and screenshots.
Wrong role/action/object fail closed in recommendation-review workflow API tests.
UI visible-text regression blocks process/proof/gate explainer copy on EPIC-11 routes.
UI tests block client-acceptance/export/download/release overclaims for S038-S042.

visual_audit: S040 release modal has visible sticky footer actions and no release-scope explainer panel.
S041 block/evidence modal has visible sticky footer actions and no current-hold explainer panel.
S042 audit view starts directly with audit readiness/status content and no redundant object-review panel.

known_warnings: NO_COLOR ignored due FORCE_COLOR appeared during Playwright/dev-server runs.
Custom Babel configuration warning appeared during Next dev server startup.
Some first parallel QA invocations failed to start because Next allows only one dev server per repo; all affected suites passed on serialized rerun.
deviations: EPIC-11 is accepted as a process-first implementation slice, not as full DOMAIN-G closure.
DOMAIN-G remains closure-blocked until backend lifecycle and audit/evidence failure proof are closed.

recommendation.reason: Close the remaining 24 step-level gate and 24 audit/evidence failure proof gaps instead of pushing partial UI proof into an implemented claim.
